title: I Love Surfing, I Hate Waking Up
description: Verbs of preference (like, love, hate, enjoy, don't mind) + verb-ING. Not 'I love surf' — 'I love surfing'.
summary_pt_br: Verbos de preferência + forma -ING: love, like, enjoy, don't mind, hate. Exemplo: 'I love SURFING' (não 'I love surf'). 'I hate WAKING UP early'.

scenes.text_en:
'I LOVE SURFING,' said Bia.
'I LIKE READING,' said Joca.
'I ENJOY COOKING,' said Rafa.
'I DON'T MIND WAKING UP early,' said Maya.
'I HATE WAITING,' said Zara.
scenes.text_pt:
'ADORO surfar,' disse a Bia.
'GOSTO de ler,' disse o Joca.
'CURTO cozinhar,' disse o Rafa.
'Não me importo de acordar cedo,' disse a Maya.
'ODEIO esperar,' disse a Zara.

scenes.title: LIKE + -ING, not bare verb
scenes.body_en: After LIKE, LOVE, HATE, ENJOY, DON'T MIND — use verb-ING. 'I like READING' (not 'I like read'). Exception: 'I like TO read' is also correct but -ING is more common. 'I would like' is different — that takes TO + base: 'I would like TO read'.
scenes.body_pt: Depois de LIKE / LOVE / HATE / ENJOY / DON'T MIND → verbo-ING. 'I love surfing'. Cuidado: 'I would like' pede TO + verbo base ('I would like TO surf').

examples.en: I would love to cook. (to + base)

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Hey. What do you like doing in your free time?
turns.pt: Ei. O que gosta de fazer no tempo livre?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I love reading books and watching movies.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, I like cooking Italian food.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, I hate waking up early.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I love swimming but I do not like running.
turns.pt_hint: 'Adoro nadar mas não gosto de correr.'

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Great idea. I love that plan.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "I Love Surfing, I Hate Waking Up". Verbs of preference (like, love, hate, enjoy, don't mind) + verb-ING. Not 'I love surf' — 'I love surfing'. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "I Love Surfing, I Hate Waking Up". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
I Love Surfing, I Hate Waking Up
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'I Love Surfing, I Hate Waking Up'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "I Love Surfing, I Hate Waking Up".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.
